---
title: "Page with Callouts and Code"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
canvas:
  type: page
  published: true
---

This page tests callout blocks and syntax-highlighted code.

## Callout Blocks

::: {.callout-note}
## Note Callout
This is a note with **bold** text and a [link](https://example.com).
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Warning Callout
Be careful with this operation.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Tip Callout
Use `pytest -v` for verbose output.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Important Callout
Do not skip this step.
:::

::: {.callout-caution}
## Caution Callout
This action cannot be undone.
:::

## Syntax-Highlighted Code

```python
def hello(name: str) -> str:
    """Greet someone."""
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

# Call the function
result = hello("Canvas")
print(result)
```

```javascript
function fibonacci(n) {
    if (n <= 1) return n;
    return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2);
}
```

## Code in Prose

Inline code like `print("hello")` should be preserved. Code blocks inside
backticks should not have their content processed as links or images:

```
![This is not an image](not_a_file.png)
[This is not a link](not_a_page.qmd)
```
